element.description: An XML element is the basic building block. Elements can contain text, attributes, and other elements.

attribute.description: Attributes provide additional information about elements. Values must be quoted.

namespace.description: XML namespaces prevent element name conflicts by qualifying names with a URI.

CDATA.description: CDATA sections contain text that should not be parsed. Useful for embedding code or special characters.

comment.description: XML comments are ignored by parsers. Cannot contain the sequence --.

declaration.description: The XML declaration specifies the XML version and character encoding. Must be the first line.

DOCTYPE.description: Document Type Declaration defines the document structure and can reference an external DTD.

processing_instruction.description: Processing instructions provide information to the application processing the XML.

well_formed.description: XML must have a single root element, properly nested tags, quoted attributes, and case-sensitive tag names.

XPath.description: XPath is a query language for selecting nodes from an XML document. Supports axes, predicates, and functions.

XSLT.description: XSLT is a language for transforming XML documents into other formats (HTML, text, other XML).

DTD.description: DTD defines the structure and legal elements/attributes of an XML document. Can be internal or external.

XSD.description: XSD is an XML-based alternative to DTD for describing the structure of XML documents with data types.
